source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/004_Clinical Image Report - G1417FW.docx

document.title: Clinical Image Report - G1417FW
document.documentType: Clinical image report / clinical evaluation attachment

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for G1417FW against F1417MCW using plain radiographic images and DICOM 3.0 image acquisition.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation of the subject device by documenting image quality comparison, exposure conditions, clinical findings, grading, and radiology expert qualifications.
keyPoints: Objective Device is identified as G1417FW by H&abyz; Comparative Equipment is F1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
Prepared, reviewed, and approved on May/16/2025 by R&D, doctor, and quality roles.
Output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used for formatting data for downloading and analysis.
Thirty radiographic studies are listed with exposure conditions and comparative image grading.
Total Average is reported as 3.99 for F1417MCW and 3.79 for G1417FW.
The conclusion states that F1417MCW has slightly better image quality and seems to have more diagnostic value.

evidence.claim: The report lists X-ray conditions for 30 studies.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray Condition NO Large group Middle group Small group kVp mAs P’T body type

evidence.claim: Image quality grading uses S, A, B, and C mapped to numeric values.
evidence.evidenceText: ( Grade S : Perfect=4 , A : excellent=3 , B : average=2 C : poor=1 )

evidence.claim: The total average image score is 3.99 for F1417MCW and 3.79 for G1417FW.
The report conclusion states that F1417MCW has slightly better image quality than G1417FW.
evidence.evidenceText: Despite the image with slight difference, F1417MCW has a little better image quality and the difference between two substances.

evidence.claim: The report conclusion states that F1417MCW seems to have more diagnostic value.
evidence.evidenceText: In conclusion, F1417MCW seems to be more of a diagnostic value.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation was conducted by a qualified medical specialist in radiology.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation of the subject device was conducted by a qualified medical specialist in the field of radiology.

evidence.claim: Dr. Wonho Chang is identified as a board-certified radiologist.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text.
No document revision or version is stated in the source text.
Text quality includes apparent OCR or transcription errors such as DCIOM 3.0, S4, Sternal Angel, and Insperction.
Some internal study labels appear inconsistent, including 2nd Finger Lat View evaluated as 2nd Finger AP View and Humerus Lat View listing Study as Humerus AP View.
Some exposure values appear inconsistent between the X-ray condition table and later study sections, including Sternum Lat and Humerus views.
